__fixture_tag__: VENDORED UNIVERSAL-FF PREDICTION — frozen NNP output, not measurement
target_property: creep life at 1050 °C / 137 MPa for a Re-free 4th-gen Ni-based single-crystal (Ru/W/Ta/Cr-balanced γ′-strengthening) plus 1000 °C oxidation rate

operating_conditions.geometry: single-crystal [001]-oriented round bar; γ + γ′ microstructure with 60-65 vol % γ′
operating_conditions.chemistry: Re-free, Ru-bearing (3-4 wt% Ru) + balanced W / Ta / Cr / Co γ′-formers
operating_conditions.oxidation_test: 1000 °C static air, 100 h cumulative, mass-gain per ASTM E2008-class protocol

model_caveat: Universal FFs predict atomistic energies / forces / stress for the γ + γ′ + topologically-close-packed (TCP) phase space of the alloy — they do NOT directly compute creep life (a long-time-scale dislocation + diffusion + microstructural-evolution property). Creep life is set by γ′ raft formation kinetics + interfacial dislocation network + Re-free TCP precipitation. The 500 h value here is a PROXY-from-literature pinned with raw#10 C3 honesty: CMSX-4-like Re-free variants tested in academic + ATI Allvac campaigns. SEMI-QUANTITATIVE — NNP frames TCP susceptibility through Md-electron parameter only.
proxy_source: Reed, Tao 2008 Acta Mater. 56:5754 (Re-free 4th-gen SX design via Md-electron / new-PHACOMP filter, predicted creep life @ 1050 °C / 137 MPa ≈ 500-700 h for Ru-balanced variants) + Reed 2006 "The Superalloys" CUP monograph baseline (CMSX-10 @ 6 wt% Re creep life ≈ 800-1000 h same conditions); ATI Allvac 2024 SX alloy development brief (Re-free experimental cast life ~ 400-550 h same conditions; preserved per `superalloy/superalloy.md` UNVERIFIED-at-parity row); Caron / Khan (ONERA) 4th-gen Re-Ru chemistry corpus 2010-2020
falsifier_text: creep life @ 1050 °C / 137 MPa < 400 h OR oxidation rate > 0.1 mg/cm²/h → FAIL
unproven_markers_preserved: Re-free 4th-gen SX UNVERIFIED at production parity preserved verbatim (per `superalloy/superalloy.md`)
TCP phase precipitation in high-W / Ta Re-free chemistry UNVERIFIED for ≥ 10⁴ h service
CMSX-10 (6 wt% Re) + René N6 (5.4 wt% Re) Re-bearing incumbents define the parity bar; Re-free SX ≥ 400 h is the floor, not parity
single-crystal grain selector yield + casting cost UNVERIFIED outside Cannon-Muskegon / PCC Airfoils (Category (c))
long-duration in-service creep + thermomechanical fatigue interaction UNVERIFIED for Re-free chemistry past 10⁴ h
honesty_caveats: Creep life 500 h CLEARS the F-NI-1 floor (≥ 400 h) and oxidation rate 0.08 mg/cm²/h CLEARS the 0.1 mg/cm²/h ceiling — but MARGINAL because the Re-bearing CMSX-10 / René N6 incumbents reach 800-1000 h at the same conditions. PASS on the falsifier floor does NOT mean Re-free parity with Re-bearing SX; the UNVERIFIED-at-parity marker stays in place per `superalloy/superalloy.md`. SPEC_FIRST preserved — does NOT promote to EXTERNAL-VERIFIED.
license_note: Peer-reviewed paper values per CC-BY publisher terms; Cannon-Muskegon master-heat + PCC Airfoils SX casting vendor authority (raw#10 C3, NOT n=6 lattice-fit).
